heading: Desertion of mails
label: 1700 Desertion of mails
text: Whoever, having taken charge of any mail, voluntarily quits or deserts the same before he has delivered it into the post office at the termination of the route, or to some known mail carrier, messenger, agent, or other employee in the Postal Service authorized to receive the same, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 322 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 19935 Stat. 1126Minor changes were made in phraseology.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $500”.Pub. L. 103–322
